rules.message: ReDoS: Regex with nested quantifiers can cause catastrophic backtracking
rules.fix: Rewrite the regex to avoid nested quantifiers (e.g., (a+)+ or (a*)*). Use atomic groups or possessive quantifiers where available, or validate input length first.

rules.message: ReDoS: Regex with overlapping alternation can cause exponential backtracking
rules.fix: Refactor the regex alternation so branches don't overlap. Consider using a trie or prefix factoring.

rules.message: ReDoS: Greedy quantifier followed by same character class risks catastrophic backtracking
rules.fix: Use possessive quantifiers or atomic groups if available. Limit input length before regex matching.

rules.message: ReDoS: User-controlled input passed to RegExp constructor without length limit
rules.fix: Escape user input with a function like escapeRegExp() before passing to new RegExp(). Also enforce a maximum length on the input.

rules.message: ReDoS: String.match/search/replace with user-controlled regex pattern
rules.fix: Never pass user input directly to .match()/.search()/.replace() as a regex. Use string methods like .includes() or .indexOf() instead.

rules.message: ReDoS: Regex with repetition of optional group (a?)+ pattern
rules.fix: Patterns like (a?)+ cause exponential backtracking. Rewrite to avoid optional elements inside repeated groups.

rules.message: ReDoS: Regex with unbounded repetition inside lookahead
rules.fix: Unbounded quantifiers inside lookaheads can cause excessive backtracking. Limit the quantifier or restructure the pattern.

rules.message: ReDoS: Regex with .* followed by character class containing dot-match
rules.fix: The pattern .*[...] where brackets overlap with dot can cause quadratic backtracking. Use more specific character classes.

rules.message: ReDoS: Regex with repeated capturing group referencing itself
rules.fix: Backreference inside a repeated group causes exponential backtracking. Restructure the pattern to avoid this.

rules.message: ReDoS: Regex with nested repetition operators (e.g., \w+\w+)
rules.fix: Adjacent quantified shorthand classes (like \w+\w+) cause quadratic behavior. Merge them into a single quantified expression.

rules.message: XSS: document.location assignment with dynamic value
rules.fix: Validate the URL against an allowlist before setting document.location. Never assign user-controlled values directly.

rules.message: XSS: window.open with user-controlled URL
rules.fix: Validate URLs passed to window.open() against an allowlist of trusted domains. Block javascript: and data: URL schemes.

rules.message: XSS: postMessage without target origin restriction
rules.fix: Replace the '*' target origin with the specific origin of the intended recipient. Using '*' allows any window to receive the message.

rules.message: XSS: message event handler without origin check
rules.fix: Always verify event.origin in message event handlers before processing the data. Without origin checks, any page can send messages to your application.

rules.message: XSS: outerHTML assignment with dynamic value
rules.fix: Use textContent for plain text or sanitize with DOMPurify before assigning to outerHTML.

rules.message: XSS: insertAdjacentHTML with dynamic content
rules.fix: Sanitize HTML content with DOMPurify before passing to insertAdjacentHTML. Use insertAdjacentText for plain text.

rules.message: XSS: document.writeln with dynamic content
rules.fix: Replace document.writeln() with DOM manipulation methods. Use textContent for text or DOMPurify for HTML.

rules.message: SSRF: axios request with user-controlled URL
rules.fix: Validate user-provided URLs against an allowlist of trusted domains. Block private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.169.254).

rules.message: SSRF: got() request with user-controlled URL
rules.fix: Validate the URL against an allowlist before making the request. Block internal/private IP ranges.

rules.message: SSRF: http.request/https.request with user-controlled options
rules.fix: Validate host/URL before making requests. Block private IP ranges and metadata endpoints.

rules.message: Cookie set without Secure flag
rules.fix: Set the Secure flag on cookies to ensure they are only sent over HTTPS.

rules.message: Cookie set without SameSite attribute
rules.fix: Set the SameSite attribute on cookies (Lax or Strict) to protect against CSRF attacks.

rules.message: Sensitive data stored in localStorage
rules.fix: Never store sensitive data in localStorage as it is accessible to any JavaScript on the page. Use HttpOnly cookies for auth tokens.

rules.message: Sensitive data stored in sessionStorage
rules.fix: Avoid storing sensitive data in sessionStorage. Use HttpOnly cookies for authentication tokens.

rules.message: JSON.parse of user input without try/catch
rules.fix: Wrap JSON.parse of user input in a try/catch block. Malformed JSON will throw and may crash your application.

rules.message: GraphQL introspection enabled in production
rules.fix: Disable GraphQL introspection in production to prevent attackers from discovering your full API schema. Enable it only in development.

rules.message: GraphQL query depth not limited
rules.fix: Add a query depth limit to your GraphQL server to prevent deeply nested queries that can cause DoS.

rules.message: Missing rate limiting on API route
rules.fix: Apply rate limiting to authentication and mutation endpoints. Use express-rate-limit, @nestjs/throttler, or similar middleware.

rules.message: WebSocket connection without origin validation
rules.fix: Validate the WebSocket connection origin using the verifyClient callback. Without origin checks, any website can connect to your WebSocket server.

rules.message: WebSocket message handler without input validation
rules.fix: Validate and parse WebSocket messages against a schema. Never trust incoming WebSocket data.

rules.message: CSRF: Form without CSRF token
rules.fix: Include a CSRF token in all forms that submit via POST. Use csurf (Express), @nestjs/csrf, or framework-specific CSRF protection.

rules.message: Unsafe file permissions: chmod 777 or 0o777
rules.fix: Never set file permissions to 777 (world-writable). Use the minimum required permissions (e.g., 0o644 for files, 0o755 for executables).

rules.message: Writing to /tmp without unique filename (symlink attack risk)
rules.fix: Use os.tmpdir() with a unique subdirectory created via fs.mkdtemp() instead of hardcoded /tmp paths. This prevents symlink attacks.

rules.message: Hardcoded IP address in source code
rules.fix: Move IP addresses to configuration files or environment variables. Hardcoded IPs make deployment and environment changes difficult.

rules.message: Stack trace exposed in error response
rules.fix: Never expose stack traces to clients in production. Return generic error messages and log stack traces server-side only.

rules.message: Insecure random number generation with Math.random()
rules.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes(), crypto.randomUUID(), or crypto.getRandomValues() for security-sensitive random values. Math.random() is not cryptographically secure.

rules.message: Math.random() used for ID generation (predictable)
rules.fix: Use crypto.randomUUID() or a library like nanoid for generating IDs. Math.random() produces predictable values.

rules.message: Deprecated crypto API: createCipher (use createCipheriv instead)
rules.fix: Use crypto.createCipheriv() with a random IV instead of the deprecated crypto.createCipher() which uses a fixed IV.

rules.message: Deprecated crypto API: createDecipher (use createDecipheriv instead)
rules.fix: Use crypto.createDecipheriv() with the correct IV instead of the deprecated crypto.createDecipher().

rules.message: Weak hash algorithm: MD5 should not be used for security purposes
rules.fix: Replace MD5 with SHA-256 or SHA-3 for integrity checks. For password hashing, use bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2.

rules.message: Weak hash algorithm: SHA-1 is deprecated for security purposes
rules.fix: Replace SHA-1 with SHA-256 or SHA-3. SHA-1 has known collision attacks.

rules.message: Weak encryption: DES/RC4 algorithms are broken
rules.fix: Use AES-256-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305 instead of DES/RC4 which are cryptographically broken.

rules.message: ECB mode encryption is insecure (patterns leak through ciphertext)
rules.fix: Use CBC, CTR, or GCM mode instead of ECB. ECB mode preserves patterns in plaintext, making it insecure.

rules.message: Hardcoded encryption key in source code
rules.fix: Load encryption keys from environment variables or a key management service. Never hardcode keys in source code.

rules.message: JWT without expiration (tokens never expire)
rules.fix: Always set an expiration time on JWTs using the expiresIn option. Tokens without expiration last forever if stolen.

rules.message: JWT signed with 'none' algorithm allows forged tokens
rules.fix: Never allow the 'none' algorithm for JWT signing. Always specify a strong algorithm like RS256 or HS256.

rules.message: CORS wildcard allows any origin
rules.fix: Replace the CORS wildcard (*) with a list of trusted origins. Wildcard CORS allows any website to make requests to your API.

rules.message: CORS with credentials and wildcard origin is a security risk
rules.fix: When CORS credentials is true, origin must be a specific domain (not wildcard). Validate origins against an allowlist.

rules.message: setTimeout/setInterval with string argument acts as eval
rules.fix: Pass a function reference to setTimeout/setInterval instead of a string. String arguments are evaluated like eval().

rules.message: Unvalidated redirect URL
rules.fix: Validate redirect URLs to ensure they are relative paths or belong to trusted domains. Block absolute URLs to prevent open redirects.

rules.message: Sensitive data in URL query parameters (may be logged or cached)
rules.fix: Send sensitive data in request headers or body, never in URL query parameters. URLs are logged by proxies, browsers, and servers.

rules.message: Unencrypted HTTP used instead of HTTPS
rules.fix: Use HTTPS instead of HTTP to encrypt data in transit. HTTP connections can be intercepted and modified.

rules.message: Child process spawned with shell option enabled
rules.fix: Avoid using shell: true with child_process. Use spawn() or execFile() with an array of arguments instead.

rules.message: Prototype pollution via Object.assign with user input
rules.fix: Sanitize user input to remove __proto__, constructor, and prototype keys before using Object.assign.

rules.message: Dynamic import with user-controlled module path
rules.fix: Never allow user input to determine which module is loaded. Use a whitelist mapping of allowed module identifiers.

rules.message: Unserialize/deserialize of untrusted data (node-serialize)
rules.fix: Never use node-serialize to deserialize untrusted data. It can execute arbitrary code. Use JSON.parse() instead.

rules.message: Express trust proxy not configured (IP spoofing risk)
rules.fix: Set 'trust proxy' to a specific number or IP range instead of true. Using true trusts all proxies and allows IP spoofing.

rules.message: Disabled TLS certificate verification (insecure)
rules.fix: Never disable TLS certificate verification in production. Fix the underlying certificate issue instead.

rules.message: Bcrypt with low cost factor (< 10 rounds)
rules.fix: Use at least 10 rounds (12+ recommended) for bcrypt. Low round counts make brute-force attacks feasible.

rules.message: Hardcoded JWT secret in source code
rules.fix: Load JWT signing secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager. Never hardcode them.

rules.message: Session stored in memory (not suitable for production)
rules.fix: Use a persistent session store (Redis, PostgreSQL, MongoDB) instead of the default memory store. Memory sessions leak memory and are lost on restart.

rules.message: File upload without size limit
rules.fix: Set file upload size limits to prevent denial-of-service attacks. Configure multer limits, formidable maxFileSize, etc.

rules.message: Missing body parser size limit
rules.fix: Set a body size limit on your JSON/URL-encoded parsers (e.g., express.json({ limit: '1mb' })) to prevent large payload DoS.

rules.message: SQL query built with string template (Knex/Sequelize raw)
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries with Knex/Sequelize. Pass values as the second argument array, not interpolated into the string.

rules.message: Lodash merge/defaultsDeep can cause prototype pollution
rules.fix: Sanitize user input before passing to lodash merge/defaultsDeep. Strip __proto__ and constructor keys.

rules.message: Axios/fetch response data used without validation
rules.fix: Validate external API response data before using it. External responses can be malformed or contain unexpected data.

rules.message: express.static serving from project root (may expose secrets)
rules.fix: Serve static files from a specific subdirectory (e.g., 'public/'), not the project root. The root may contain .env files, source code, and configuration.

rules.message: Disabled HTTPS redirect (app running on HTTP)
rules.fix: Use HTTPS in production. Create an HTTPS server or use a reverse proxy (nginx, Cloudflare) that terminates TLS.

rules.message: Debug mode enabled in production framework
rules.fix: Disable debug mode in production. Debug mode often exposes internal details, stack traces, and request data.

rules.message: Error message exposes internal implementation details
rules.fix: Return generic error messages to clients. Log detailed errors server-side only.

rules.message: Insecure password hashing: raw SHA/MD5 used for passwords
rules.fix: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2 for password hashing. SHA and MD5 are too fast and vulnerable to brute-force attacks.

rules.message: Django model field without validators
rules.fix: Add validators to Django model fields to enforce data integrity at the model level.

rules.message: Flask route without authentication decorator
rules.fix: Add @login_required or equivalent authentication decorator to protected routes.

rules.message: Jinja2 autoescape disabled (XSS risk)
rules.fix: Set autoescape=True (or use select_autoescape) in Jinja2 Environment to prevent XSS.

rules.message: Insecure YAML loading: yaml.load without SafeLoader
rules.fix: Use yaml.safe_load() or yaml.load(data, Loader=yaml.SafeLoader) instead of yaml.load(). Unsafe YAML loading can execute arbitrary code.

rules.message: Insecure tempfile: using mktemp (race condition)
rules.fix: Use tempfile.mkstemp() or tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile() instead of mktemp(). mktemp is vulnerable to race conditions.

rules.message: Path traversal: os.path.join with user input
rules.fix: Validate that the joined path stays within the expected directory using os.path.commonpath() or pathlib. Reject input containing '..'.

rules.message: Command injection: subprocess with shell=True
rules.fix: Use subprocess with shell=False and pass arguments as a list. shell=True allows command injection.

rules.message: Hardcoded Django admin URL at /admin/
rules.fix: Use a non-default admin URL path. Attackers routinely scan for /admin/ to find Django admin panels.

rules.message: Missing CSRF protection in Django view
rules.fix: Avoid using @csrf_exempt. If you must disable CSRF for an API, use token-based authentication (e.g., DRF with TokenAuthentication).

rules.message: Insecure pickle: loading untrusted data with pickle
rules.fix: Never unpickle data from untrusted sources. Pickle can execute arbitrary code on deserialization. Use JSON or another safe format.

rules.message: Django DEBUG=True should not be used in production
rules.fix: Set DEBUG=False in production. Django's debug mode exposes detailed error pages with source code, SQL queries, and settings.

rules.message: Django SECRET_KEY hardcoded in settings
rules.fix: Load SECRET_KEY from an environment variable. A leaked SECRET_KEY allows session forgery and other attacks.

rules.message: SQL injection via Python string formatting
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries with %s placeholders and pass values as a tuple. Never use f-strings or .format() for SQL.

rules.message: Flask app.run with debug=True in production code
rules.fix: Never use debug=True in production. Use a WSGI server (gunicorn, uwsgi) instead of the development server.

rules.message: Python eval() with external input
rules.fix: Never use eval() with user input. Use ast.literal_eval() for safe evaluation of data structures, or JSON.loads() for JSON.

rules.message: Python exec() can execute arbitrary code
rules.fix: Never use exec() with untrusted input. Use safe alternatives like JSON parsing, configuration files, or sandboxed execution.

rules.message: Django ALLOWED_HOSTS wildcard allows host header attacks
rules.fix: Set ALLOWED_HOSTS to specific domain names. Using '*' allows host header injection attacks.

rules.message: Insecure SSL context: verification disabled
rules.fix: Never disable SSL certificate verification in production. Fix the certificate chain issue instead.

rules.message: Flask session secret key is weak or hardcoded
rules.fix: Generate a strong random secret key using os.urandom(32) or secrets.token_hex(32). Load it from an environment variable.

rules.message: Dangerous default mutable argument in function
rules.fix: Use None as the default and create a new list/dict inside the function. Mutable default arguments are shared between calls.

rules.message: Django raw SQL query with string formatting
rules.fix: Use Django ORM methods or parameterized raw queries: Model.objects.raw('SELECT * FROM t WHERE id = %s', [user_id]).

rules.message: Insecure XML parsing (XXE vulnerability)
rules.fix: Use defusedxml library instead of the standard xml module. The standard library XML parsers are vulnerable to XXE attacks.

rules.message: Hardcoded database password in source code
rules.fix: Load database passwords from environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.message: Python logging with sensitive data
rules.fix: Mask sensitive data before logging. Use structured logging with sensitive field filtering.

rules.message: Django SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT not enabled
rules.fix: Set SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True in production to redirect all HTTP requests to HTTPS.

rules.message: Open redirect in Django/Flask redirect
rules.fix: Validate redirect URLs using Django's url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme() or manual hostname checks.

rules.message: Mass assignment in Django model form (fields = '__all__')
rules.fix: Explicitly list the allowed fields instead of using '__all__'. This prevents users from modifying sensitive fields like 'is_admin'.

rules.message: Python hashlib without proper salt for password hashing
rules.fix: Use bcrypt, argon2, or Django's make_password() for password hashing. Raw hash functions are too fast for passwords.

rules.message: Django CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE not enabled
rules.fix: Set CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True in production to send CSRF cookies only over HTTPS.

rules.message: Django SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE not enabled
rules.fix: Set SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True in production to send session cookies only over HTTPS.

rules.message: Hardcoded API credentials in Python source
rules.fix: Load API credentials from environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.message: Python requests library without timeout
rules.fix: Always set a timeout on requests to prevent hanging indefinitely: requests.get(url, timeout=30).

rules.message: CORS allow all origins in Flask
rules.fix: Configure CORS with specific allowed origins instead of allowing all origins.

rules.message: SQLAlchemy text() with string formatting
rules.fix: Use SQLAlchemy's text() with :param syntax and pass values as bound parameters.

rules.message: Celery task accepting arbitrary executable code
rules.fix: Never execute arbitrary code inside Celery tasks. Use specific task functions with validated inputs.

rules.message: Django file upload without content type validation
rules.fix: Add file extension and content-type validators to Django file upload fields.

rules.message: Python __import__ with user input
rules.fix: Never use __import__ with user input. This allows arbitrary module loading and code execution.

rules.message: Django template with autoescape off
rules.fix: Avoid disabling autoescape in Django templates. Use the |safe filter on specific trusted values instead.

rules.message: Unvalidated redirect in Go HTTP handler
rules.fix: Validate redirect URLs against an allowlist. Only allow relative paths or specific trusted domains.

rules.message: Missing error check on database operation
rules.fix: Always check errors returned from database operations. Unchecked errors can mask data corruption or injection.

rules.message: Go cookie without Secure flag
rules.fix: Set Secure: true on cookies to ensure they are only sent over HTTPS.

rules.message: Go cookie without HttpOnly flag
rules.fix: Set HttpOnly: true on cookies to prevent JavaScript access and mitigate XSS-based cookie theft.

rules.message: Using text/template instead of html/template for HTML output
rules.fix: Use html/template instead of text/template when generating HTML. html/template automatically escapes values to prevent XSS.

rules.message: Goroutine sharing variable without synchronization
rules.fix: Use channels, mutexes, or atomic operations for goroutine synchronization. Shared mutable state without synchronization causes race conditions.

rules.message: Missing context timeout on database/HTTP operations
rules.fix: Use context.WithTimeout() or context.WithDeadline() instead of context.Background() for operations that should time out.

rules.message: Exposed pprof endpoint in production
rules.fix: Remove or gate pprof imports behind a build tag or environment check. Exposed pprof endpoints leak goroutine stacks and memory profiles.

rules.message: SQL injection via string concatenation in Go
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries with $1/$2 or ? placeholders. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings.

rules.message: Command injection in Go via os/exec with user input
rules.fix: Validate and sanitize user input before passing to exec.Command. Use the command and args separately (not as a shell string).

rules.message: Go TLS InsecureSkipVerify disables certificate validation
rules.fix: Never set InsecureSkipVerify to true in production. Fix the certificate chain or trust store issue instead.

rules.message: Go weak TLS version (TLS 1.0 or 1.1)
rules.fix: Set MinVersion to tls.VersionTLS12 or tls.VersionTLS13. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 have known vulnerabilities.

rules.message: Go http.ListenAndServe without TLS
rules.fix: Use http.ListenAndServeTLS() for production servers, or put the server behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy.

rules.message: Go HTTP handler without timeout configuration
rules.fix: Set ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, and IdleTimeout on http.Server to prevent slowloris and resource exhaustion attacks.

rules.message: Unsafe use of reflect in Go
rules.fix: Use type assertions and type switches instead of reflect when possible. Reflection bypasses type safety.

rules.message: Go SSRF: http.Get with user-controlled URL
rules.fix: Validate user-provided URLs against an allowlist. Block private IP ranges and cloud metadata endpoints.

rules.message: Go filepath.Join with user input (path traversal)
rules.fix: Validate that the joined path stays within the expected directory. Check with filepath.Rel() or strings.HasPrefix().

rules.message: Go unsafe package usage
rules.fix: Avoid the unsafe package unless absolutely necessary. It bypasses Go's memory safety guarantees.

rules.message: Go CORS wildcard origin
rules.fix: Configure CORS with specific allowed origins instead of wildcard. Wildcard allows any website to make requests to your API.

rules.message: Go bcrypt with low cost factor
rules.fix: Use bcrypt.DefaultCost (10) or higher. Low cost factors make brute-force attacks feasible.

rules.message: Missing CSRF protection in Go web handler
rules.fix: Add CSRF protection using gorilla/csrf, nosurf, or similar middleware for POST/PUT/DELETE handlers.

rules.message: Go JWT without expiration claim
rules.fix: Always set an expiration claim on JWTs. Tokens without expiration last forever if stolen.

rules.message: Go md5/sha1 used for security-sensitive hashing
rules.fix: Use sha256 or sha512 for security-sensitive hashing. MD5 and SHA-1 have known collision attacks.

rules.message: Go os.MkdirAll with permissive permissions
rules.fix: Use restrictive permissions (e.g., 0755 or 0700) for os.MkdirAll. 0777 allows any user to read/write/execute.

rules.message: Go defer inside loop (resource leak risk)
rules.fix: Move defer out of loops or wrap the loop body in a function. Deferred calls in loops accumulate until the function returns.

rules.message: Go HTTP response body not closed (resource leak)
rules.fix: Always defer resp.Body.Close() after checking for errors on HTTP requests.

rules.message: Go crypto/rand not used for random bytes
rules.fix: Use crypto/rand instead of math/rand for security-sensitive random values. math/rand is deterministic.

rules.message: Go fmt.Sprintf used in SQL query construction
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries instead of fmt.Sprintf for SQL. This prevents SQL injection.

rules.message: Go Gin/Echo/Fiber route without auth middleware
rules.fix: Add authentication middleware to protected routes.

rules.message: Go log.Fatal in HTTP handler (crashes the server)
rules.fix: Use log.Printf or return an error instead of log.Fatal in HTTP handlers. Fatal calls os.Exit which crashes the entire server.

rules.message: Terraform resource missing tags
rules.fix: Add tags to all AWS resources for cost tracking, ownership, and compliance.

rules.message: Terraform S3 bucket without logging enabled
rules.fix: Enable S3 access logging to track who accesses your bucket and when.

rules.message: Terraform RDS without backup retention
rules.fix: Set backup_retention_period to at least 7 days for RDS databases.

rules.message: Terraform RDS instance publicly accessible
rules.fix: Set publicly_accessible = false on RDS instances. Databases should not be directly accessible from the internet.

rules.message: Terraform security group allows all inbound traffic (0.0.0.0/0)
rules.fix: Restrict inbound traffic to specific IP ranges. Only allow 0.0.0.0/0 for ports 80/443 on load balancers.

rules.message: Terraform EBS volume not encrypted
rules.fix: Set encrypted = true on EBS volumes to encrypt data at rest.

rules.message: Terraform RDS without encryption at rest
rules.fix: Set storage_encrypted = true on RDS instances to encrypt data at rest.

rules.message: Terraform CloudTrail logging disabled
rules.fix: Keep CloudTrail logging enabled. It provides an audit trail of all API calls in your AWS account.

rules.message: Terraform IAM policy with wildcard actions
rules.fix: Follow the principle of least privilege. Specify only the required IAM actions instead of using wildcard (*).

rules.message: Terraform Lambda function without VPC configuration
rules.fix: Consider placing Lambda functions in a VPC if they access private resources. Add vpc_config with appropriate subnets and security groups.

rules.message: Terraform S3 bucket without versioning
rules.fix: Enable S3 bucket versioning to protect against accidental deletion and allow recovery of previous versions.

rules.message: Terraform S3 bucket without encryption
rules.fix: Enable server-side encryption on S3 buckets with aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.

rules.message: Kubernetes pod missing liveness probe
rules.fix: Add a livenessProbe to containers to detect and restart hung processes.

rules.message: Kubernetes pod missing readiness probe
rules.fix: Add a readinessProbe to containers to prevent routing traffic to pods that aren't ready.

rules.message: Kubernetes hostPath volume mount (security risk)
rules.fix: Avoid hostPath volumes in production. They break pod isolation and can expose host filesystem to containers.

rules.message: Kubernetes pod using default service account
rules.fix: Create a dedicated service account with minimal RBAC permissions instead of using the default service account.

rules.message: Kubernetes container running as root
rules.fix: Run containers as a non-root user. Set runAsUser to a non-zero UID and runAsNonRoot: true.

rules.message: Kubernetes privileged container
rules.fix: Never run containers in privileged mode in production. Privileged containers have full host access.

rules.message: Kubernetes container without resource limits
rules.fix: Set resource limits (CPU and memory) on containers to prevent resource exhaustion.

rules.message: Kubernetes pod with host network access
rules.fix: Avoid hostNetwork: true. Pods with host network access can sniff traffic and access network services on the host.

rules.message: Kubernetes secret in environment variable (prefer volume mount)
rules.fix: Mount secrets as volumes instead of environment variables. Environment variables may be logged or exposed via /proc.

rules.message: Kubernetes container allows privilege escalation
rules.fix: Set allowPrivilegeEscalation: false to prevent processes from gaining more privileges than their parent.

rules.message: Kubernetes pod with host PID namespace access
rules.fix: Avoid hostPID: true. Containers with host PID access can view and signal all processes on the host.

rules.message: Kubernetes container image using 'latest' tag
rules.fix: Use specific image tags or SHA digests instead of 'latest'. Latest tags are mutable and can change unexpectedly.

rules.message: Kubernetes NetworkPolicy not defined
rules.fix: Define NetworkPolicies to restrict pod-to-pod communication. Without policies, all pods can communicate freely.

rules.message: Kubernetes ClusterRoleBinding to cluster-admin
rules.fix: Avoid binding to the cluster-admin ClusterRole. Create roles with minimum required permissions.

rules.message: Docker multi-stage build may leak secrets from build stage
rules.fix: Use Docker build secrets (--mount=type=secret) instead of ARG/ENV for sensitive values. ARG values are stored in image layers.

rules.message: pip install without --no-cache-dir wastes image space
rules.fix: Add --no-cache-dir to pip install commands in Dockerfiles to reduce image size.

rules.message: Docker COPY or ADD of entire context (may include secrets)
rules.fix: Use a .dockerignore file to exclude .env, .git, node_modules, and other sensitive files. Or COPY specific files instead of the entire context.

rules.message: Docker container running as root (no USER instruction)
rules.fix: Add a USER instruction to run the container as a non-root user. Running as root increases the blast radius of container escapes.

rules.message: Docker EXPOSE on privileged port without justification
rules.fix: Use non-privileged ports (>1024) in containers. Privileged ports require root or special capabilities.

rules.message: Docker ADD used instead of COPY (ADD has extra features that may be unexpected)
rules.fix: Use COPY instead of ADD for local files. ADD auto-extracts archives and supports URLs, which can introduce unexpected behavior.

rules.message: Docker apt-get without cleanup (increases image size)
rules.fix: Chain apt-get update, install, and cleanup in a single RUN layer: RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends pkg && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

rules.message: Docker image using latest tag (non-reproducible builds)
rules.fix: Pin Docker base images to specific version tags or SHA digests for reproducible builds.

rules.message: GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch without input validation
rules.fix: Add input definitions with types and validation to workflow_dispatch triggers.

rules.message: GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN with excessive permissions
rules.fix: Follow the principle of least privilege. Set specific permissions (e.g., contents: read, issues: write) instead of write-all.

rules.message: GitHub Actions using outdated/unpinned third-party action
rules.fix: Pin third-party GitHub Actions to a specific commit SHA instead of a branch or tag. This prevents supply chain attacks.

rules.message: GitHub Actions secret exposed in run command
rules.fix: Pass secrets via environment variables instead of directly in run commands. Direct use may expose them in logs.

rules.message: GitHub Actions pull_request_target with checkout of PR code
rules.fix: pull_request_target runs with write access to the base repo. Checking out and running PR code can lead to code injection. Use pull_request event instead or add strict permission limits.

rules.message: GitHub Actions artifact upload without retention limit
rules.fix: Set retention-days on artifact uploads to avoid accumulating storage costs.

rules.message: GitHub Actions shell injection via github context in run
rules.fix: Never use github event data directly in run commands. Assign to an env variable first to prevent shell injection.

rules.message: GitHub Actions workflow without concurrency control
rules.fix: Add a concurrency group to prevent duplicate workflow runs on the same branch.

rules.message: TODO/FIXME comment mentions security concern
rules.fix: Address security-related TODO/FIXME comments before deploying to production.

rules.message: Commented-out security code detected
rules.fix: Commented-out security code suggests protection was disabled. Either restore it or remove it with an explanation.

rules.message: Hardcoded localhost URL in production code
rules.fix: Use environment variables or configuration files for service URLs. Hardcoded localhost URLs won't work in production.

rules.message: Hardcoded development/staging URL in non-test file
rules.fix: Use environment variables for service URLs. Hardcoded staging URLs should not be in production code.

rules.message: Potential password in variable assignment
rules.fix: Load passwords from environment variables, a secrets manager, or a secure configuration source.

rules.message: Potential API key in variable assignment
rules.fix: Load API keys from environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.message: Connection string with embedded credentials
rules.fix: Load database connection strings from environment variables. Embedded credentials in source code are easily leaked.

rules.message: Private key content embedded in source code
rules.fix: Never embed private keys in source code. Load them from files, environment variables, or a key management service.

rules.message: Disabled security feature via comment flag
rules.fix: Review disabled security linter rules. Suppressed security warnings should have a clear justification comment.

rules.message: Hardcoded AWS region (should be configurable)
rules.fix: Load AWS region from environment variables or configuration. Hardcoded regions make multi-region deployment harder.

rules.message: Base64-encoded string that may be a credential
rules.fix: Base64 is encoding, not encryption. Move credentials to environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.message: IP address allowlist bypassed or commented out
rules.fix: Commented-out IP allowlist code suggests security was disabled. Restore or properly replace the IP filtering.

rules.message: Source map file referenced in production code
rules.fix: Remove source map references from production builds. Source maps expose your original source code.

rules.message: Disabled authentication check
rules.fix: Commented-out authentication checks leave endpoints unprotected. Restore the auth check or implement proper access control.

rules.message: Wildcard import may include more than intended
rules.fix: Use explicit imports instead of wildcard (*) imports. Wildcard imports make it hard to track where names come from and can cause name collisions.

rules.message: Weak comparison operator (== instead of ===) may cause type coercion issues
rules.fix: Use strict equality (===) instead of loose equality (==) for security-sensitive comparisons to prevent type coercion bypasses.

rules.message: Non-constant-time string comparison for secrets
rules.fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual() for comparing secrets. Regular comparison (===) is vulnerable to timing attacks.

rules.message: Global error handler may swallow security errors
rules.fix: Ensure uncaughtException handlers log the error and exit the process. Swallowing errors can hide security issues.

rules.message: Directory listing may be enabled (express.static without index)
rules.fix: Disable directory listing in express.static by setting { index: false } and consider using { dotfiles: 'deny' }.

rules.message: XML external entity (XXE) processing enabled
rules.fix: Disable external entity processing in XML parsers to prevent XXE attacks.

rules.message: Potential timing attack in authentication comparison
rules.fix: Use constant-time comparison for authentication secrets. Regular string comparison leaks information through timing.

rules.message: Disabled eslint security rule
rules.fix: Review disabled eslint security rules. If suppressing is necessary, add a comment explaining why.

rules.message: Debug logging enabled unconditionally
rules.fix: Gate debug logging behind environment checks. Debug logs may expose sensitive data in production.

rules.message: Potential command injection via backtick in shell script
rules.fix: Use $() instead of backticks for command substitution, and always quote variables to prevent injection.

rules.message: Unquoted variable in shell script (word splitting risk)
rules.fix: Always double-quote shell variables to prevent word splitting and globbing attacks.

rules.message: Shell script without set -e (errors not caught)
rules.fix: Add 'set -euo pipefail' to the top of shell scripts to catch errors, unset variables, and pipe failures.

rules.message: curl without certificate verification (-k or --insecure)
rules.fix: Remove -k/--insecure from curl commands. Fix the underlying SSL certificate issue instead.

rules.message: wget without certificate verification (--no-check-certificate)
rules.fix: Remove --no-check-certificate from wget commands. Fix the certificate issue instead.

rules.message: SSH with disabled host key checking
rules.fix: Don't disable SSH host key checking. Add the host key to known_hosts instead.

rules.message: Exposed Swagger/OpenAPI documentation endpoint
rules.fix: Protect Swagger/OpenAPI endpoints in production. Exposed API documentation helps attackers understand your API surface.

rules.message: Potential open redirect via URL parameter
rules.fix: Validate redirect URLs. Only allow relative paths starting with '/' (but not '//') or URLs matching an allowlist of trusted domains.

rules.message: Potential log injection via user input in log message
rules.fix: Sanitize user input before logging to prevent log injection. Remove newlines and control characters.

rules.message: HTTP header injection via user input
rules.fix: Validate and sanitize user input used in HTTP headers. Strip newlines (\r\n) to prevent header injection.

rules.message: Sensitive file extension served by static handler
rules.fix: Configure express.static with { dotfiles: 'deny' } to prevent serving .env, .git, and other dotfiles.

rules.message: Insecure direct object reference (IDOR): resource accessed by user-provided ID without authorization
rules.fix: Verify that the requesting user has permission to access the resource. Check ownership or role-based access before returning data.

rules.message: HTML form action points to external URL
rules.fix: Verify that form actions pointing to external URLs are intentional. This could be a phishing vector.

rules.message: iframe without sandbox attribute
rules.fix: Add the sandbox attribute to iframes to restrict their capabilities. Use allow-scripts, allow-forms, etc. selectively.

rules.message: Markdown rendering without sanitization (XSS risk)
rules.fix: Sanitize rendered markdown with DOMPurify before inserting into the DOM. Markdown can contain arbitrary HTML and script tags.

rules.message: Server-sent event stream without authentication
rules.fix: Add authentication to SSE endpoints. Without authentication, anyone can subscribe to your event stream.

rules.message: S3 bucket name hardcoded (should be configurable)
rules.fix: Load S3 bucket names from environment variables or configuration files.

rules.message: Recursive function without depth limit
rules.fix: Add a depth/recursion limit to recursive functions to prevent stack overflow on malicious input.

rules.message: Unsafe YAML parsing in JavaScript
rules.fix: Use yaml.load with SAFE_SCHEMA or the safe API (js-yaml safe functions). Unsafe YAML parsing can execute arbitrary code.

rules.message: Server version or technology exposed in response headers
rules.fix: Remove or hide server version headers (X-Powered-By, Server). They help attackers identify your technology stack.

rules.message: Insecure cookie configuration: cookie domain too broad
rules.fix: Avoid setting cookies on broad domains. Use the most specific domain possible to limit cookie scope.

rules.message: Potential CRLF injection in HTTP header value
rules.fix: Sanitize user input in HTTP headers by removing \r and \n characters to prevent CRLF injection.

rules.message: eval() executes arbitrary code. Never use with untrusted input.
rules.fix: Remove eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or a safe expression evaluator.

rules.message: new Function() is equivalent to eval(). Avoid with untrusted input.
rules.fix: Remove new Function(). Use safe alternatives like JSON.parse() or a sandboxed evaluator.

rules.message: document.write() can cause XSS and destroys existing DOM content
rules.fix: Use DOM APIs (createElement, appendChild, textContent) instead of document.write()

rules.message: MD5 is cryptographically broken. Use SHA-256 or better.
rules.fix: Replace createHash('md5') with createHash('sha256') or createHash('sha512')

rules.message: SHA-1 is deprecated and vulnerable to collision attacks. Use SHA-256 or better.
rules.fix: Replace createHash('sha1') with createHash('sha256') or createHash('sha512')

rules.message: Redirect target should be validated against an allowlist
rules.fix: Validate the redirect URL against a known allowlist to prevent open redirect attacks

rules.message: cors() with no arguments allows all origins — restrict to known domains
rules.fix: Pass an options object: cors({ origin: 'https://yourdomain.com' })

rules.message: CORS with wildcard origin allows any website to make requests
rules.fix: Replace wildcard origin with specific allowed domains

rules.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML bypasses React's XSS protection
rules.fix: Sanitize HTML with DOMPurify before using dangerouslySetInnerHTML

rules.message: JWT signing — ensure secret is from environment, not hardcoded
rules.fix: Use an environment variable for the JWT secret: jwt.sign(payload, process.env.JWT_SECRET)

rules.message: JWT verification — ensure secret matches signing key and algorithm is pinned
rules.fix: Pin the algorithm: jwt.verify(token, secret, { algorithms: ['HS256'] })

rules.message: exec() runs shell commands. Never pass unsanitized user input.
rules.fix: Use execFile() with an argument array instead of exec() with a command string

rules.message: execSync() runs shell commands synchronously. Avoid with user input.
rules.fix: Use execFileSync() with an argument array, or validate/escape the command

rules.message: fs.readFileSync with dynamic path may allow path traversal
rules.fix: Validate and normalize file paths with path.resolve() and check they stay within allowed directories

rules.message: fs.writeFileSync with dynamic path may allow arbitrary file write
rules.fix: Validate and normalize file paths; ensure they stay within an allowed directory

rules.message: express.json() without size limit allows request body DoS
rules.fix: Add a size limit: express.json({ limit: '1mb' })

rules.message: Cookie set without security options (httpOnly, secure, sameSite)
rules.fix: Set security options: res.cookie(name, value, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'strict' })

rules.message: Direct assignment to window.location can enable open redirect attacks
rules.fix: Validate the URL against a known allowlist before redirecting

rules.message: SQL query with string concatenation inside an arrow function
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries: db.query('SELECT * WHERE id = $1', [id])

rules.message: eval() without error handling
rules.fix: Remove eval entirely, or at minimum wrap in try/catch

rules.message: Cookie set without httpOnly security option
rules.fix: Add httpOnly: true to cookie options

rules.message: fs.readFileSync inside an exported function may block the event loop
rules.fix: Use fs.promises.readFile() or fs.readFile() with a callback instead of blocking I/O in handlers

rules.message: jwt.sign with a hardcoded string secret
rules.fix: Use an environment variable or key management service for the JWT secret

rules.message: exec/execSync inside an arrow function handler may allow command injection
rules.fix: Use execFile or execFileSync with an argument array instead of exec with string interpolation

rules.message: exec() inside an arrow function handler may allow command injection
rules.fix: Use execFile with an argument array instead of exec with string interpolation

rules.message: cursor.execute() with f-string or format string — SQL injection risk
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * WHERE id = %s', (id,))

rules.message: os.system() executes shell commands — use subprocess with shell=False
rules.fix: Use subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg'], shell=False) instead of os.system()

rules.message: pickle.load/loads deserializes arbitrary objects — code execution risk
rules.fix: Use JSON, MessagePack, or another safe serialization format instead of pickle

rules.message: yaml.load() without SafeLoader can execute arbitrary code
rules.fix: Use yaml.safe_load() or yaml.load(data, Loader=yaml.SafeLoader)

rules.message: eval() executes arbitrary code — never use with untrusted input
rules.fix: Use ast.literal_eval() for safe evaluation of literals, or avoid eval entirely

rules.message: db.Query/Exec with string concatenation — SQL injection risk
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries: db.Query('SELECT * WHERE id = $1', id)

rules.message: exec.Command runs shell commands — validate input carefully
rules.fix: Avoid passing user input to exec.Command. Use an allowlist of permitted commands.

rules.message: InsecureSkipVerify disables TLS certificate validation
rules.fix: Remove InsecureSkipVerify: true — validate TLS certificates in production

rules.message: http.ListenAndServe without TLS — use ListenAndServeTLS for production
rules.fix: Use http.ListenAndServeTLS() with valid certificates for production deployments

rules.message: fmt.Sprintf used to build SQL query — SQL injection risk
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries instead of fmt.Sprintf for SQL statements

rules.message: ActiveRecord .where with string interpolation — SQL injection risk
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries: Model.where('col = ?', value) or .where(col: value)

rules.message: system() executes shell commands — validate input carefully
rules.fix: Use array form system('cmd', 'arg') to avoid shell injection, or validate input

rules.message: Marshal.load deserializes arbitrary objects — code execution risk
rules.fix: Use JSON.parse or a safe serialization format instead of Marshal.load

rules.message: YAML.load can execute arbitrary code — use YAML.safe_load
rules.fix: Use YAML.safe_load(data) instead of YAML.load(data)

rules.message: params.permit! allows all parameters — mass assignment vulnerability
rules.fix: Use params.permit(:field1, :field2) to explicitly allowlist permitted parameters

rules.message: DES encryption is broken and should not be used
rules.fix: Use AES-256-GCM or AES-256-CBC instead of DES.

rules.message: RC4 cipher is broken and should not be used
rules.fix: Use AES-256-GCM instead of RC4.

rules.message: Blowfish cipher is weak. Use AES instead.
rules.fix: Use AES-256-GCM instead of Blowfish.
